title: asclepias emoryi
intro: Asclepias emoryi, also known as Emory's milkweed or Whitestem butterfly milkweed, is a perennial plant species within the milkweed family (Apocynaceae) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and act as a natural antiseptic. The health benefits of Asclepias emoryi include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce stress, and improve respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used topically to treat wounds and skin irritations.
